name: Tyler Technologies Munis ERP API
what_it_does: Tyler Technologies Munis Government ERP REST API for comprehensive public sector financial management, HR, and procurement. Enables AI agents to manage general ledger and fund accounting for government financial reporting, handle budget development and management for government fiscal planning, access accounts payable and purchasing automation for government procurement, retrieve payroll and human resources management for public sector workforce, manage utility billing and accounts receivable for government revenue collection, handle grant management and federal reporting for public sector grant compliance, access fixed asset management and depreciation for government capital accounting, retrieve financial reporting and CAFR preparation for government audit compliance, manage project accounting and cost allocation for government capital projects, and integrate Munis with Tyler's Brazos, Incode, New World, and other public sector platforms.
use_cases: Automating government payroll from HR agents
Managing budget transfers from finance agents
Processing vendor payments from AP agents
Generating CAFR reports from compliance agents
not_for: Private sector organizations without government accounting
Federal agencies beyond state and local scope
International governments outside US public sector
best_when: A US state or local government using Tyler Munis ERP wants AI agents to automate general ledger, payroll, purchasing, grant management, utility billing, and financial reporting.
avoid_when: GASB GOVERNMENT ACCOUNTING RISK: Automated journal entries and financial adjustments must comply with GASB (Government Accounting Standards Board) fund accounting principles; automated general ledger entries without GASB compliance review create CAFR inaccuracies and state audit findings. Government payroll compliance — automated public sector payroll must handle union contracts (CBA), PEBA retirement contributions, FICA exemptions for some government employees, and state-specific public employee benefit plans; automated payroll with incorrect government-specific rules creates payroll liability. Grant compliance automation — automated federal grant drawdown and reporting must comply with Uniform Guidance (2 CFR Part 200) including allowable cost determination and subrecipient monitoring; automated grant accounting without Uniform Guidance compliance creates federal audit risk.

auth.notes: Tyler Munis uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes via Tyler Developer Network. REST API with JSON. Documentation at developer.tylertech.com. Plano, Texas HQ. NASDAQ: TYL. Founded 1966. Public company. 22,000+ government customers. Munis is Tyler's flagship ERP for mid-size government. Tyler also offers New World ERP, Incode, and Brazos for different government segments. Dominant in US local government ERP.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — government annual subscription
pricing.notes: Plano, Texas. NASDAQ: TYL. Founded 1966. Public company. 22,000+ government customers. Tyler dominant in US local government ERP. Munis flagship for mid-size government.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; government SLA documented

known_agent_gotchas: GASB FUND ACCOUNTING: Automated journal entries in government general ledger must respect GASB fund accounting principles; automated entries that violate fund integrity (e.g., inappropriate interfund transfers) create CAFR inaccuracies and state audit findings

Public employee payroll complexity — government payroll includes union CBA rules, PEBA/PERS retirement contributions, FICA exemptions for some government employees, and state-specific benefit plans; automated payroll must implement government-specific rules, not commercial payroll logic

Federal grant Uniform Guidance — automated federal grant accounting must comply with 2 CFR Part 200 allowable cost determination; automated grant drawdown without allowability verification creates federal audit findings and potential grant recovery

Budget appropriation controls — Munis enforces budget appropriation limits; automated AP transactions that exceed appropriations are rejected; implement budget availability checking before automated purchase order creation
StateRAMP authorization — state agencies increasingly require StateRAMP authorization for cloud services; verify current Tyler Munis StateRAMP status for state agency compliance requirements
Tyler platform ecosystem complexity — Tyler offers Munis, New World ERP, Incode, and Brazos for different government segments; verify Munis-specific API documentation applies to your target platform
agent_readiness.security_notes: Government ERP. SOC2, StateRAMP. OAuth2 with scopes. US. Government financial and HR data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Government ERP. SOC2, StateRAMP. OAuth2 with scopes. US. Government financial and payroll data.
